onboarding.question:
1. Define what "without visual disruption" means in concrete, measurable terms — specify exact timing constraints (e.g., indicator disappears within one frame), opacity behavior, or other observable markers of a seamless transition.
2. Clarify whether the session expiry warning in Criterion 8 is dismissible or non-dismissible — Criterion 15 implies dismissal is possible, but Criterion 8 states non-dismissible; resolve this contradiction and specify the exact user experience.
3. Complete Criterion 23 or remove it.
4. Specify the user-facing experience when SSO fails or the user cancels authentication — what message appears, what options are available, and whether the user can retry or return to the logged-out chat.
onboarding.productSpec:
# Onboarding — Product Spec

## Problem
New and returning users need a frictionless path from first landing to the main chat home screen. Unauthenticated users risk losing their conversation history without realizing it, which undermines trust and conversion. The onboarding experience must get users to the home screen quickly, make the value of signing in clear without forcing it, and ensure no one is surprised by data loss.

## Target Users
- **New users** — arriving for the first time, no existing account
- **Returning users** — have an existing account, signing back in
- **Logged-out new users** — new users who choose to explore before signing up

## User Stories
1. As a new user, I want to sign up quickly so that I can start using Health360 without unnecessary friction.
2. As a returning user, I want to sign in and land directly on the home screen so that I can pick up where I left off.
3. As a logged-out user, I want to explore the product before committing to sign up so that I can understand its value first.
4. As a logged-out user, I want a subtle, persistent indicator that I am not signed in so that I am never surprised by the implications of being logged out.
5. As a logged-out user, I want to be warned before my session expires so that I can sign up and save my conversation before it's lost.
6. As a logged-out user, I want to be warned when I try to navigate away or close the tab so that I don't accidentally lose my conversation.
7. As a logged-out user, I want to know that signing in will preserve my conversation so that I have a clear reason to create an account at that moment.
8. As a new user completing sign-up from a logged-out session, I want my existing conversation to carry over so that I don't lose the context I've already built.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Unauthenticated users can access the chat interface and interact with the AI without signing in.
2. A persistent, non-dismissible indicator is visible to logged-out users displaying the text "Not signed in".
3. The indicator is read-only, displays no interactive elements, does not overlay other UI, and does not trigger any prompts or modals on its own.
4. The logged-out indicator remains visible at all times while the user is not authenticated.
5. New users can sign up via SSO (Google, Apple Sign-In) only — no password-based registration.
6. Returning users can sign in via SSO and are taken directly to the main chat home screen upon successful authentication.
7. New users who complete sign-up are taken directly to the main chat home screen upon successful authentication.
8. When a logged-out user's session is approaching expiry (at 10 minutes remaining) AND they have at least one conversation exchange, a non-dismissible warning banner appears at the top of the chat interface with the message "Your session will expire in 10 minutes. Sign in to save your conversation" and a "Sign in now" call-to-action button; the warning is cleared if the user performs an action that resets the inactivity timer (e.g., sends a message), which restarts the session countdown.
9. When a logged-out user attempts to navigate away or close the tab AND they have at least one conversation exchange, a modal is surfaced with the message "Your conversation will be lost if you leave without signing in", a primary "Sign in" button, and a secondary "Leave anyway" button; the modal has no dismiss affordance (no X button or click-outside-to-close) and the user must choose one of the two buttons to proceed; clicking "Leave anyway" permits the user to proceed with navigation or tab closure.
10. After the first complete AI response (full message streamed and rendered) in a session, an in-conversation nudge surfaces informing the logged-out user that their conversation will not be saved unless they sign in, rendered as a conversational system message with a clear sign-up or sign-in call-to-action.
11. The in-conversation nudge fires exactly once per session and does not repeat on subsequent exchanges.
12. Both warnings and the in-conversation nudge include a clear call-to-action to sign up or sign in.
13. Upon completing sign-up or sign-in from any warning CTA, the user is taken directly to the main chat home screen and their previous logged-out conversation is immediately visible in the message thread without requiring additional navigation or explicit retrieval; system warning messages and the in-conversation nudge are excluded from the carried-over conversation thread.
14. If the logged-out user has no conversation history, no session expiry or navigation warning is shown.
15. If the user dismisses a warning and does not sign in, the session expires and the conversation is lost — no further prompts.
16. There is no forced sign-up wall at any point in the logged-out experience.
17. The path from landing page to authenticated home screen requires a maximum of 3 user interactions: (1) landing on the logged-out chat interface, (2) tapping a sign-in or sign-up call-to-action, (3) completing SSO and being taken directly to the authenticated home screen. No intermediate profile setup, goal-setting, or confirmation screens are permitted.
18. Goal setting does not occur during onboarding — it happens post-onboarding through the AI conversation.
19. On initial page load, the UI defaults to logged-out mode immediately while authentication state resolution occurs in the background.
20. If authentication state resolution succeeds within 2 seconds of initial load, the user transitions to authenticated state without visual disruption; the logged-out indicator disappears silently and conversation history loads progressively into the existing message thread without page reload, skeleton screens, spinners, or opacity changes; any conversation the user started during the auth resolution window is treated as part of the logged-out session and is carried over to the authenticated home screen's message thread.
21. If authentication state resolution fails or exceeds 2 seconds, the user remains in logged-out mode and a retry affordance appears inline at the top of the chat interface; the retry affordance is dismissible and persists until the user attempts retry or navigates away from the page.
22. Unauthenticated sessions expire after 60 minutes of inactivity; each user action (message send, keystroke, tap) resets the inactivity timer back to 60 minutes.
23.

## Edge Cases

- User signs up from the warning CTA but the session expires before authentication completes — conversation cannot be recovered; surface a clear, friendly message.
- User begins sign-up flow from the warning but abandons it — session continues to tick down

## Non-Goals
- Goal setting, profile building, and AI coaching — these begin after the user reaches the home screen.
- Password-based authentication — SSO only.
- Forced sign-up walls or conversion prompts outside of the defined warning triggers and in-conversation nudge.
- Auto-saving or server-side backup of logged-out conversations without explicit user action.
- Changing session expiry duration — infrastructure concern outside this spec.
- Social or referral flows — not part of the initial vision.

## Design Direction
**Dark mode primary — Archon Labs aesthetic (getarchon.dev).** Visual language: minimal, high negative space. The interface recedes; the conversation leads. Chrome is nearly invisible until needed.

**Color palette (extracted from getarchon.dev):**
- Background: `#0A0E27` (dark navy, almost black)
- Surfaces: `#151B38` (fractionally lighter, subtle elevation)
- Text primary: `#F5F5F5` (off-white, full contrast on dark background)
- Text muted: `rgba(245, 245, 245, 0.6)` (neutral grey for secondary labels)
- Accent gradient: 135° from `#8B7FE8` (soft violet) to `#4FADA8` (muted teal)

**Signature treatment: Pulsing glow effect**
- Radial gradient: violet-teal (same palette as accent gradient)
- Animation: async heartbeat pulse (not synchronized to user input or other glow instances)
- Opacity cycle: 25% → 35% → 25% over 2.5 seconds, ease-in-out cubic bezier (cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1))
- Position: fixed behind UI elements (prompt bar and Auth Sheet buttons)
- Applied in exactly two places: (1) chat home background behind prompt bar, (2) Auth Sheet behind SSO buttons
- Behavior as conversation fills: blur radius shrinks (200px → 100px), opacity reduces (25–35% → 12–16%) over ~4 messages or 60% viewport fill
- Minimum state: 12–16% opacity, ~100px blur radius — still present, clearly visible
- Each glow instance pulses independently — no synchronization between home glow and Auth Sheet glow
- Nowhere else in the product
- Conveys: ambient warmth, alive-ness without distraction

## Open Questions
(none)
